templates.name: Incident Detected
templates.template: Incident '{incident_id}' (Severity {severity}) created by '{owning_tenant_name}': '{title}'. Created: {create_date}, Impact start: {impact_start_date}. Customer may be impacted.
templates.extracted_when: An incident exists in the dataset for the relevant time window, regardless of outage confirmation status.

templates.name: Confirmed Outage
templates.template: Incident '{incident_id}' (Severity {severity}) by '{owning_tenant_name}' is a confirmed outage: '{title}'. Impact started: {impact_start_date}. {child_count} child incidents reported.
templates.extracted_when: The incident has IsOutage set to true, confirming customer-facing impact. This is the strongest outage signal.

templates.name: Outage Time Overlap with SLI Breach
templates.template: Incident '{incident_id}' (impact start: {impact_start_date}) overlaps with SLI breach on subscription '{subscription_id}' in region '{region}' (breach start: {earliest_impact_start}). Time delta: {delta_minutes} minutes. This temporal correlation suggests the incident caused the SLI degradation.
templates.extracted_when: An incident's ImpactStartDate falls within a reasonable time window of an SLI breach's EarliestImpactStart, indicating temporal correlation between platform incident and customer SLI impact.

templates.name: Customer-Correlated Incident
templates.template: Incident '{incident_id}' (Severity {severity}) by '{owning_tenant_name}' is linked to support ticket '{support_ticket_id}': '{title}'. Impact started: {impact_start_date}. This directly correlates a platform incident with a customer complaint.
templates.extracted_when: The incident has a non-empty SupportTicketId, directly linking a platform incident to a customer's support request.
